name: Proto-Mongolic (family node)
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Proto-Mongolic is the reconstructed common ancestor of the Mongolic family; Middle Mongol is its earliest attested stage. It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Janhunen, J. (ed.)
sources.title: The Mongolic Languages
sources.notes: Reference for Mongolic branching (structural node only).
